blocks.content: Cassandra pulls back just enough to look at her.
Lena shakes her head slightly — not dismissing it, just waiting for her to finish.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: No — I mean it. All of it. Every waiting room you sat in without being asked. Every time you didn't push me when I wasn't ready. Every time you just — sat with me. Knowing, and not making it bigger than I could hold, and trusting me to get here when I got here.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You knew before I did that I could do this. I didn't believe you. I do now.

Lena's eyes are bright. She holds it together the way she always holds things together — by choice, with great effort, with the specific dignity of a person who has decided that this moment belongs to someone else.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: You didn't think I'd be anywhere else.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I'm so proud of you, Cass.
Cassandra nods. She can't answer that one. She just nods.
